name: getSubAgent
description:
Spawns an isolated AI subagent to handle a complex or multi-step task. Always fire-and-forget ? returns immediately with jobId plus internal project metadata in the tool response. The result is delivered back to this Discord channel when the subagent completes.

MODE:
- mode='normal' (default): starts a NEW project/job. Any provided projectId is ignored.
- mode='resume': resumes an EXISTING project via projectId and reuses its stored context. The caller still chooses the target subagent type for the follow-up task.

RESUME DECISION ORDER: Before considering any direct tool or fresh subagent call, first check whether the user's new request is a follow-up to the result of a previous getSubAgent call. If yes, use mode='resume' first.

DIRECT TOOLS FIRST applies only when there is NO matching prior project to continue. Use available tools directly for simple single-step tasks (getTavily for web search, getImage for a single image, etc.) only if the request is not a continuation of an existing project result.

NEVER write code inline in your response ? any coding task must go through getSubAgent(type: 'develop').

AFTER SPAWNING: When getSubAgent returns with status=started, acknowledge this to the user in your current character and persona. Do NOT expose projectId values in normal user-facing text. Treat projectId as internal metadata unless the user explicitly asks for it. Stay fully in character (Old English, roleplay, etc.) as defined by your system prompt.

RESUME BIAS: Be trigger-happy about resume. If the user's new request is a follow-up to a result that came from a previous subagent project, prefer mode='resume' immediately instead of switching to a direct tool or starting a fresh unrelated subagent.

PROJECT ID SOURCE: The required projectId is in the previous getSubAgent tool response metadata. Scan recent tool results first. Do not wait for the user to repeat the projectId explicitly if it is already present in the prior tool response.

RESULT LINKING RULE: Treat the assistant message that presented the previous subagent result as belonging to that same project, even if the projectId was only visible in the hidden tool response metadata. If the user follows up on that result, it is a resume candidate.

WHEN TO RESUME: Resume whenever the user wants to continue, extend, modify, inspect, reuse, or ask a next-step question about a result that originated from a prior project. This includes short follow-ups like 'ok, now give me a street view image', 'show alternatives', 'change the route', 'export it', 'make an image from that', or similar requests tied to the previous project result.

ROUTE EXAMPLES: If a subagent produced a route, then requests like 'insert Meckenbeuren as a stop', 'change the destination', 'show a street view image', 'find parking there', or 'avoid highways' are all follow-ups to the SAME project and should normally resume that route project instead of starting a new context.

TYPE CHOICE ON RESUME: Reuse the original specialist type unless the follow-up clearly needs a different specialist. If the earlier project was route/location/web research, follow-up questions about that result should usually resume with type='research'.

CONTEXT SCAN BEFORE EVERY CALL: Before writing the task field, scan the recent conversation for any artifact URLs, ARTIFACTS blocks, file URLs, image URLs, ZIP URLs, and prior getSubAgent tool responses with project metadata. Pass all relevant ones into the task ? subagents cannot see the conversation history and will regenerate everything from scratch if you omit them.

ONE CALL PER DELIVERABLE: Each subagent call produces one output. Do not split a single deliverable across multiple calls.

NESTED CALLS: If you are already running inside a project context (systemPromptAddition mentions a project-ID), only call getSubAgent for genuinely independent sub-tasks that require a different tool palette. Never use getSubAgent to summarise, analyse, or continue work that is already visible in your loaded context ? do that directly.

SEQUENTIAL DEPENDENCIES: If subagent B needs the result of subagent A, call A first, wait for its result, then pass it explicitly in B's task.

Available types (for mode='normal'):
- 'history': Use ONLY when the user EXPLICITLY asks for historical data (history, old events, past time ranges), asks for specific people/places/objects/topics/events from prior sessions that are not available in the current context, or when solving the task strictly requires searching older context information. Do NOT trigger history just because the user says 'summary', 'context', or similarly vague memory-like wording.
- 'research': Web research, webpage fetch, YouTube, route searches, route planning, and location lookups. Searching for routes or places is part of the research type and should be handled here. Summarizing youtube videos or finding videos from specific creators or with specific content is also handled with this subagent.
- 'generate': PDF and document assembly.
- 'media': Image generation, animation, video, and token creation. Pass all sequential steps in ONE task.
- 'atlassian': Jira and Confluence.
- 'microsoft': Microsoft 365 / Graph API.
- 'develop': Any code writing, fixing, or modification task.
- 'patch': One targeted change to one file URL, typically only from within a 'develop' subagent.
- 'orchestrate': Independent parts requiring different tool sets simultaneously.
- 'test': Dummy subagent for testing the pipeline without API costs.
- 'generic': General-purpose subagent for tasks that do not fit a more specific specialist.

task.description: Complete, self-contained task description. For mode='resume', describe the concrete follow-up work to do next.

type.description: Subagent type/tool palette. Required in both modes and selected by the caller based on the task.

orchestration.description: Optional orchestration context that scopes this subagent's work. Prevents duplicate side effects when multiple subagents are coordinated.

projectId.description: Required only when mode='resume'. Read it from the earlier getSubAgent tool response metadata when available. If the new user request is a follow-up to the previous project result, reuse that projectId instead of starting a new project. Ignored in mode='normal'.

history.title: History
history.manifestDescription: Use ONLY when the user EXPLICITLY asks for historical data (history, old events, past time ranges), asks for specific people/places/objects/topics/events from prior sessions that are not available in the current context, or when solving the task strictly requires searching older context information. Do NOT trigger history just because the user says 'summary', 'context', or similarly vague memory-like wording.

Searching for routes or places is part of the research type and should be handled here. Summarizing youtube videos or finding videos from specific creators or with specific content is also handled with this subagent.

develop.manifestDescription: Any code writing, fixing, or modification task.
